floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: outer volatile is LOAD-BEARING — cse.c source proves the intervening u16 pointer-stores cannot invalidate a fixed-address scalar global entry, so the path-C re-read folds without it

frontier.hypothesis: Retype the CD-register pointer cluster (D_800A147C..D_800A1490) as one struct (or array) of volatile u16*/u8* register pointers so g_cd_spu_voice loads are MEM_IN_STRUCT_P; drop the outer volatile; the path-C re-read then survives CSE naturally
frontier.mechanism: cse.c:1715 — invalidate_memory removes in_struct entries when any nonscalar write occurs; the v1[...] u16 stores are nonscalar (PLUS address), so the global's cached equivalence dies before the second read
frontier.next_probe: sandbox cdrom_ConfigSPU --disable all with the struct retype (scoped to system.c decls); then FULL verify-oracle — the retype touches other cd functions' loads (687-689, 710-716 etc.) and must be byte-neutral TU-wide

frontier.hypothesis: Control probe: drop ONLY the outer volatile (keep everything else) and confirm the predicted divergence (second lui/lw pair disappears, path-C join uses stale $v1) — pins the exact instruction delta the lever must recreate and proves the flag is not stale
frontier.mechanism: cse fold of the second (mem (symbol_ref)) load per the analysis above
frontier.next_probe: sandbox with inner-volatile-only decl; expect non-zero with the 720B4 lui/lw pair missing

frontier.hypothesis: Provenance check: if system.c's cdrom_* layer matches PsyQ libcd (CVS-tagged bios.c v1.86 is linked in this EXE), the outer volatile may be Sony's original declaration — authentic source semantics, closable by allowlist citation instead of restructuring
frontier.mechanism: original-source authenticity: volatile in the vendor's source is not coercion; volatile_extern_allowlist.txt is the tracking mechanism
frontier.next_probe: diff cdrom_ConfigSPU/cdrom_Shutdown/cdrom_Initialize shapes against PsyQ libcd disassembly/known libcd source (decomp.me corpus, psy-q SDK headers) for an exact-provenance call

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Unauthorized outer-volatile on pointer variable g_cd_spu_voice (src/system.c:691): declared `extern volatile u16 * volatile g_cd_spu_voice;` — the OUTER volatile makes the pointer VALUE itself volatile, forcing GCC to re-read the pointer from 0x800A1490 on every access. The data section (asm/data/7D920.data.s:31040) confirms g_cd_spu_voice holds the constant 0x1F801C00 (SPU hardware base); a search of all asm funcs found no stores to 0x800A1490 at runtime — it is never modified by any IRQ handler or other code. g_cd_spu_voice is NOT in volatile_extern_allowlist.txt; no IRQ-writer citation was ever filed. The outer volatile is what preserves the second `lw $v1, D_800A1490` at target asm offset 720B4 (the `v1 = g_cd_spu_voice;` re-read at src/system.c:700 inside the inner if). Without it, GCC CSEs the first pointer load and elides the second. Introduced in early commit 5f97a9a9 (2026-04-10) before the inline-asm-policy ban and allowlist mechanism existed. Worker redo: remove the outer volatile (keep `extern volatile u16 *g_cd_spu_voice;` — inner volatile for MMIO is correct), then find the pure-C structure that produces the second `lw D_800A1490` at asm offset 720B4 without volatile coercion on the pointer variable (e.g. access g_cd_spu_voice directly each time without a cached local, or restructure conditionals so GCC naturally spills/reloads).  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
